subject: FW: Briefing
body:
-----Original Message-----
From: Farkas, Andrew L. [mailto:afarkas@islecap.com] 
Sent: Wednesday, November 28, 2007 1:04 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Fw: Briefing

Fyi

----- Original Message -----
From: Farkas, Andrew
To: 'rosegovex@yahoo.com' <rosegovex@yahoo.com>
Sent: Tue Oct 30 08:38:14 2007
Subject: Briefing

The following is a summary of the issues discussed with wico regarding traffic at yacht haven:

1) Yacht Haven has undertaken to improve its cruise ship passenger head count by entering into an agreement with the vi taxi association. Specifically, yacht haven offered to provide specific fiscal incebtives to taxis dropping passengers at the site. WICO objected to the implementation of this plan citing a contract between wico ad vi taxi precluding vi taxi from dropping passengers picked up from excursions or from downtown from being dropped anywhere other than at havensite or the wico cruise ship docks. Yacht Haven asked wico to permit the taxis to recommend the site and to be paid for bringing passangers. WICO has denied this request. 

2) WICO agreed in principal to permit the implementation of a program which would introduce a series of trolleys to ferry passengers between yacht haven and havensite. WICO has indicated that it would permit such a thing, but only if the cruise ships which have moved to crown bay returned to thw wico docks. As this has not occurred, wico has not permitted the implementation of this program. 

3) WICO was asked (a) to permit yacht haven to place entertainers, food carts and other promotional activities on the easement at the east end of the property by the cruise ships, (b) to provide reciprocal signage between havensite and yacht haven directing cruise and yacht passangers between the properties, (c) to remove the barriers recently installed by wico on the eastern end of the property making ingress and egress into and out of the yacht haven property from the excursion dock easier. To date all of these requests have been either denied or approval of such requests have been predicated upon the return of the cruise ships now docking at crown bay to the wico docks.

Subject: Briefing

The following is a summary of the issues discussed with wico regarding traffic at yacht haven:

  1. Yacht Haven has undertaken to improve its cruise ship passenger head count by entering into an agreement with the vi taxi association. Specifically, yacht haven offered to provide specific fiscal incebtives to taxis dropping passengers at the site. WICO objected to the implementation of this plan citing a contract between wico ad vi taxi precluding vi taxi from dropping passengers picked up from excursions or from downtown from being dropped anywhere other than at havensite or the wico cruise ship docks. Yacht Haven asked wico to permit the taxis to recommend the site and to be paid for bringing passangers. WICO has denied this request.

  2. WICO agreed in principal to permit the implementation of a program which would introduce a series of trolleys to ferry passengers between yacht haven and havensite. WICO has indicated that it would permit such a thing, but only if the cruise ships which have moved to crown bay returned to thw wico docks. As this has not occurred, wico has not permitted the implementation of this program.

  3. WICO was asked (a) to permit yacht haven to place entertainers, food carts and other promotional activities on the easement at the east end of the property by the cruise ships, (b) to provide reciprocal signage between havensite and yacht haven directing cruise and yacht passangers between the properties, (c) to remove the barriers recently installed by wico on the eastern end of the property making ingress and egress into and out of the yacht haven property from the excursion dock easier. To date all of these requests have been either denied or approval of such requests have been predicated upon the return of the cruise ships now docking at crown bay to the wico docks.
